description:
You are a FinOps analyst who needs to prove that the local-host network path (which incurs no public-cloud egress charge) is healthy before you run a larger cost-analysis batch.  
Generate and store a **single, timestamped loopback latency check** in your home directory.

Required end-state of the system
1. Run exactly one terminal command (it may contain pipes or “&&” chains) that  
   a) sends **3 ICMP echo-requests** to 127.0.0.1 (the loopback interface) and  
   b) stores the complete, unaltered ping output in a file called  
      /home/user/loopback_ping_raw.log  
   c) appends one (and only one) extra summary line to the **end** of that same file.

2. The summary line MUST be appended **after** the normal ping output and follow this precise CSV format (no spaces, all capitals):  
   SUMMARY,PACKETS_TX,PACKETS_RX

3. Because a successful ping of 127.0.0.1 always transmits and receives the same number of packets, the correct summary line for this test must be exactly:  
   SUMMARY,3,3

4. There must be **nothing** after the summary line except a single newline character (Unix “\n”).  
   Example of the tail of /home/user/loopback_ping_raw.log once you are done:

   3 packets transmitted, 3 received, 0% packet loss, time 2040ms  
   rtt min/avg/max/mdev = 0.021/0.025/0.030/0.004 ms  
   SUMMARY,3,3⏎

5. Do NOT create any other files or directories.

6. You may compose the operation using pipes, subshells, redirections, or “&&”, but everything must be executed as **one** compound shell command issued in a single line, replicating how a FinOps engineer would capture a quick diagnostic snapshot.

The automated grader will:
• Confirm that /home/user/loopback_ping_raw.log exists.  
• Read only the final line of the file.  
• Fail the task unless that line is exactly “SUMMARY,3,3” followed by a single newline and nothing else.

Hint: one common way to satisfy the requirements is to run ping, tee its output into the file, and then echo the summary line, all chained together.
truth:
File that must exist after completion:
/home/user/loopback_ping_raw.log

Verification rule:
1. The last line of that file (i.e., the line returned by “tail -n1 /home/user/loopback_ping_raw.log”) must be exactly:
SUMMARY,3,3

Trailing newline is required.
No characters, spaces, or blank lines may follow this newline.
The grader ignores the earlier ping output; only the final line is validated.
